questions.question: In a connectivity graph, the number of vertices that can be removed without disconnecting the graph is called its _______________________ connectivity. Two vertices can be removed if and only if the graph is still connected.

In a connectivity graph, the minimum number of edges that need to be removed to disconnect the graph is called its _______________________ connectivity. If a graph has n vertices, its edge connectivity is at least n-1.

Consider a connectivity graph with 5 vertices and 6 edges. If removing any one of the edges results in the graph becoming disconnected, which of the following sets is a cut set for this graph? This set should contain a total of 2 edges to disconnect the graph.
